title: Missing slippage protection in deposit function
reason: The function is atomic and always mints exactly `amount` in the same transaction; there is no intra-transaction rebase window. The real issue is stale 1:1 mint pricing before an admin-triggered rebase, which is already captured separately.

title: Missing slippage protection in withdraw function
reason: Queued withdrawals intentionally track rebasing shares via `requestedRestakedTokenShares` and convert them at processing time. Amount drift from later rebases is the designed accounting model, not a distinct slippage bug.

title: Unbounded loop in _delegatorExists causes DoS
reason: The loop is bounded by the admin-controlled `maxDelegators` cap and is only exercised from admin-only delegator management. No permissionless protocol-level DoS is shown.

title: Unbounded loop in rebaseInfo causes DoS
reason: This iteration is likewise bounded by `maxDelegators`, which governance sets before adding delegators. It is an administrative scalability limit, not a reportable permissionless DoS on users.

title: Unchecked return value in payDirect
reason: `Utils.payDirect` uses `SafeERC20.safeTransfer`, which reverts on failure. The returned `true` is redundant but does not mask failed transfers.

title: Missing validation allows setting staked token to non-whitelisted
reason: Changing whitelist status is a privileged configuration action, and existing holders are not locked by this alone because `withdraw()` does not check the whitelist flag.

title: Incorrect access control on processWithdrawals
reason: `processWithdrawals()` is explicitly protected by `onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE)`.

title: Integer overflow in totalWithdrawalRequests
reason: The contract is compiled with Solidity 0.8.9, so arithmetic overflow reverts instead of wrapping.

title: Missing underflow protection in claim function
reason: The subtraction uses Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic and will revert on underflow. No independent manipulation path was shown.

title: Missing zero address check in setStakedTokenMapping
reason: `Utils.contractExists()` is required for all configured addresses, and the zero address fails that check.

title: Potential reentrancy through token callback
reason: `deposit()` is protected by `nonReentrant`, and the candidate does not show a viable callback path that bypasses it.

title: Insufficient validation of delegator index in critical functions
reason: `removeDelegator()` already checks `_delegatorIndex < delegators.length` before mutating the array.

title: Missing deadline parameter in deposit/withdraw
reason: A missing deadline is generic transaction UX/slippage hygiene, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in this codebase.

title: Missing validation for duplicate delegator addition attempt
reason: Duplicate delegators are explicitly rejected by `_delegatorExists()`. The remaining concern is only loop cost, which is bounded by `maxDelegators`.

title: Admin can change eigenLayerStrategyManagerAddress to malicious contract
reason: This is ordinary trusted-governance risk under an existing admin role, not a vulnerability in the permission model or implementation.

title: No access control on completeQueuedWithdrawal legacy function
reason: The function only reads `withdrawals[msg.sender]` and also checks `withdrawalInfo.withdrawer == msg.sender`, so callers cannot complete arbitrary other users' withdrawals.

title: Missing check for stale data in claim function
reason: `claim()` loads the caller's stored request, requires `PROCESSED` status, and updates both storage copies before transferring funds. No realistic stale-data exploitation path was demonstrated.

title: Inconsistent token balance validation in restakeDelegator
reason: The arithmetic is checked under Solidity 0.8, so a bad balance condition reverts instead of creating silent corruption. This is not a distinct exploitable issue.

title: Missing event for critical initialization values
reason: Event coverage is an observability concern, not a reportable security finding.

title: Inconsistent revert messages
reason: Inconsistent revert strings are not a security issue.
